items.title: Building Capabilities for a Multi-Agent System with Google ADK, MCP, and Cloud Run
items.snippet: A practical guide to building capabilities for a multi-agent system with Google ADK, MCP, Cloud Run deployment, and supporting security guidance.
items.content:
This tutorial walks through a multi-agent system that uses Google ADK, MCP, and Cloud Run to coordinate tools and capabilities.

## System Overview
The multi-agent system coordinates specialist agents and shared tool access.

## Google ADK Capabilities
Google ADK defines capabilities and agent behaviors.

## MCP Integration
MCP provides tool contracts, routing, and capability boundaries.

## Cloud Run Deployment
Cloud Run hosts the capability services and the agent runtime.

## Security and Credentials
Credentials and access boundaries are covered as supporting operational guidance.

items.title: Best MCP Gateways for Enterprise Teams in 2026
items.snippet: A deep technical analysis of MCP gateways, request routing, capability boundaries, and one Google Cloud knowledge-tool section.
items.content:
This article explains MCP gateway internals for tool-using agents, including request routing, capability boundaries, and delegated access.

## Architecture
The gateway sits between agent callers and tool adapters.

## MCP Gateway
The MCP gateway coordinates tool calls and capability boundaries.

## OAuth Tokens
OAuth token exchange protects one delegated access flow in the gateway.

## Policy Enforcement
Security and authorization rules are applied before tool execution.

## Google Cloud Docs (Knowledge Tool)
One knowledge tool uses Google Cloud Docs as a document source.

The MCP gateway is the central mechanism throughout the article.

items.title: How to Authorize AI Agents Using Token Exchange Open Standards
items.snippet: An architecture and security article on authorization, token exchange, and policy boundaries for AI agents.
items.content:
This article explains authorization, token exchange, and security controls for AI agents using OAuth-style standards and policy boundaries.

## Authorization Model
The authorization model defines permissions and delegated access.

## Token Exchange
Token exchange creates scoped access for agent workflows.

## Policy Boundaries
Security policies and access-control boundaries protect tool execution.

items.title: Congrats to the OpenClaw Challenge Winners!
items.snippet: A community roundup celebrating challenge winners, event highlights, and a few brief agent-project mentions from the week.
items.content:
This community update highlights challenge winners, an event recap, and a few brief project references.

## Winners
The winners are announced with short notes.

## Event Recap
The event recap highlights talks and demos.

## Project Mentions
A few agent projects are mentioned in passing.

items.title: I Built My Mom an AI Recipe Helper for Mother's Day
items.snippet: A personal project using OpenAI, a small app interface, and cloud hosting to suggest recipes and shopping ideas.
items.content: This personal project uses AI prompts to suggest recipes and shopping ideas. The app runs in the cloud and includes a lightweight workflow for generating meal plans. It does not discuss agents, orchestration, tool use, delegation, or memory beyond a passing note about conversation history.
